accessibility artifact-reference example extensions guideline link metadata rich-text status usecase
anatomy api component design-specifications state variant
theme token-api token-value token
style
pattern

DSDS Status Definition

The lifecycle status of a design system artifact and optional platform-specific readiness tracking. Used across all DSDS document types to indicate maturity and readiness.

Source: common/status.schema.json

3 definitions in this file:

  • status
  • platformStatusEntry
  • platformStatus

status

The lifecycle status of the artifact. Standard values: 'draft' (under development, not ready for use), 'experimental' (available but API may change without notice), 'stable' (production-ready, changes follow semver), 'deprecated' (scheduled for removal). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case (e.g., 'sunset', 'archived', 'beta').

string

platformStatusEntry

The readiness status of an artifact on a specific platform. Tracks lifecycle stage, version availability, and optional deprecation notices independently per platform.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
statusstatusrequiredThe lifecycle status of the artifact on this platform. Uses the same vocabulary as the top-level status: 'draft', 'experimental', 'stable', 'deprecated'. Custom values are permitted.
sincestringoptionalThe version in which this artifact became available on this platform.
deprecationNoticestringoptionalRequired when status is 'deprecated'. Explains what to use instead on this platform.
descriptionstringoptionalOptional notes about the artifact's status on this platform (e.g., 'Available as a Web Component wrapper; native implementation planned for v4').

Conditional: When status is "deprecated", then deprecationNotice is required.

References: status

platformStatus

Platform-specific readiness or availability status for an artifact. Keys are platform identifiers and values describe the artifact's status on that platform. Platform keys are freeform strings — common values include 'react', 'web-component', 'ios', 'android', 'flutter', 'figma', 'sketch', and 'compose'. When present, the top-level `status` property represents the overall lifecycle stage of the artifact, while `platformStatus` provides granular per-platform detail. An artifact MAY be 'stable' overall while individual platforms are still 'experimental' or 'draft'.

object

Open map: keys are strings, values are any

References: platformStatusEntry
